members.name: BufferLength
members.summary: The length of the buffer to keep, in seconds. Higher values keep data around for longer, but require more memory. Value can range from 0.1 to 4.

members.name: FftSize
members.summary: The size of the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fast_Fourier_transform]Fast Fourier transform[/url] buffer. Higher values smooth out the spectrum analysis over time, but have greater latency. The effects of this higher latency are especially noticeable with sudden amplitude changes.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_1024
values.summary: Use a buffer of 1024 samples for the Fast Fourier transform. This is a compromise between latency and stability over time.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_2048
values.summary: Use a buffer of 2048 samples for the Fast Fourier transform. High latency, but stable over time.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_256
values.summary: Use a buffer of 256 samples for the Fast Fourier transform. Lowest latency, but least stable over time.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_4096
values.summary: Use a buffer of 4096 samples for the Fast Fourier transform. Highest latency, but most stable over time.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_512
values.summary: Use a buffer of 512 samples for the Fast Fourier transform. Low latency, but less stable over time.

values.name: FFT_SIZE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum FFTSize] enum.
